evidence_quotes.quote: protections for customers from publishers destroying already purchased video games post purchase

paragraphs.text: Dear officials, I am Dominik Mesarić from Croatia. I would kindly like to give feedback and suggestions for the digital fairness act. Coming from the stop killing games initiative that has reached more then 1.4 million signatures I am looking forward having a voice. I would kindly request for consideration the following: - protections for customers from publishers destroying already purchased video games post purchase with a rule that would protect consumers free will to play the game whenever they desire, even if it is in 50 or 60 years time. - protections from capital interest of credit card and payment processors that have market monopoly from influencing the video game market as a whole and being able to dictate specific examples of types of games a consumer is allowed and not allowed to consume. I feel both of these issues firmly breach too deep into my individual life and other EU citizens. That being said, I would kindly request your official power to protect video games, protect consumers and firmly defend our love for video games. Lastly, the current act as it is is also nice as there are things in it that I feel should be regulated. Thank you for reading.
